metadata.title: TextArea
metadata.description: Collect longer text content from users with a multi-line input that expands automatically.

properties.description: A hint as to the intended content of the field. When set to on (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents. When set to off, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes. Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.
The default value for the field.

Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
A unique identifier for the element.
Content to use as the field label.
Changes the visibility of the component's label. visible: the label is visible to all users.exclusive: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.
Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.
Specifies the min number of characters allowed.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.
A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.
The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
A number of visible text lines.
The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-text-area
  label="Shipping address"
  value="1776 Barnes Street, Orlando, FL 32801"
  rows="3"
></s-text-area>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_area :shipping_address, label: "Shipping address", value: "1776 Barnes Street, Orlando, FL 32801", rows: "3" %>

examples.name: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple text area for collecting product descriptions with a placeholder and autocomplete disabled.
examples.html_code:
<s-text-area
  label="Product description"
  placeholder="Enter a detailed description..."
  autocomplete="off"
></s-text-area>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_area :product_description, label: "Product description", placeholder: "Enter a detailed description...", autocomplete: "off" %>

examples.name: Seo meta description with character limit
examples.description: Showcases a text area for writing SEO meta descriptions with a character limit of 160, providing guidance text and a multi-line input.
examples.html_code:
<s-text-area
  label="Meta description"
  maxLength="160"
  details="Appears in search results. Keep under 160 characters for best visibility."
  placeholder="Write a compelling description that will appear in Google search results..."
  rows="3"
  autocomplete="off"
></s-text-area>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_area :meta_description, label: "Meta description", maxlength: "160", details: "Appears in search results. Keep under 160 characters for best visibility.", placeholder: "Write a compelling description that will appear in Google search results...", rows: "3", autocomplete: "off" %>

examples.name: With error state
examples.description: Demonstrates the error state of a text area with a minimum length requirement, showing how validation errors are displayed in a commerce context.
examples.html_code:
<s-text-area
  label="Reason for return"
  error="Please provide a detailed explanation for the return request. This helps us improve our products and process the refund faster."
  minLength="20"
  placeholder="Explain why the customer is returning this item..."
  rows="3"
  autocomplete="off"
></s-text-area>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_area :reason_for_return, label: "Reason for return", error: "Please provide a detailed explanation for the return request. This helps us improve our products and process the refund faster.", minlength: "20", placeholder: "Explain why the customer is returning this item...", rows: "3", autocomplete: "off" %>

examples.name: Product care instructions
examples.description: Illustrates a text area for entering detailed product care instructions, with an expanded height and supporting guidance text.
examples.html_code:
<s-text-area
  label="Care instructions"
  rows="6"
  details="Detailed care instructions help customers maintain their purchases and reduce returns."
  placeholder="Provide washing, storage, and maintenance instructions..."
  autocomplete="off"
></s-text-area>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.text_area :care_instructions, label: "Care instructions", rows: "6", details: "Detailed care instructions help customers maintain their purchases and reduce returns.", placeholder: "Provide washing, storage, and maintenance instructions...", autocomplete: "off" %>
